nodes.label: P4.4 — class count (A5) + real-data anchors
nodes.rationale: Competitive-but-trails axis: on harsh synthetic the gap-to-BP widens with class count (+0.06->+0.23), but real anchors (digits +0.027, CIFAR-flat +0.090) show the trail is difficulty-driven, not count-driven — real flat data is far kinder than the synthetic sweep.

nodes.label: A5 — static class-count is a trail axis, not a win axis
nodes.rationale: On the harsh controlled synthetic the gap-to-tuned-BP widens with class count (+0.06 to +0.23, saturating ~C10) and OURS's edge over Mono-Forward erodes by C20 — but this conflates class count with discrimination hardness at fixed cluster geometry, not a pure count penalty.

nodes.label: Real anchors (digits, CIFAR-flat) show the class-count gap is difficulty-driven, not count-driven
nodes.rationale: On real digits (10-class, well-separated clusters) the gap is only +0.027 versus the synthetic C10 gap of +0.23 — 'many classes' is not OURS's downfall; the 40-cluster random-label synthetic discrimination is simply a hard read favouring supervised BP.
